title: QPB harness plan
description: Input to `qpb_harness_tick.py --init`. One run per entry. dispatch_mode is 'subagent' (in-session, cadence rung 1) or 'shell' (detached host-CLI, cadence rungs 2-4 / the ticker). Top-level knobs default in the tick script if omitted.

tick_interval_minutes.description: Minutes the orchestrator waits between ticks (ScheduleWakeup cadence) while work is in flight.

pool_size.description: Max concurrent in-flight runs (claimed/running/stalled). Subscription concurrency caps may force a lower value.

stall_threshold_minutes.description: A run whose heartbeat mtime is older than this is marked stalled. The mandatory ~3-min worker keepalive keeps a live run well under it.

launch_grace_minutes.description: A claimed run that emits NO heartbeat within this window is marked auth_or_launch_failed (the dispatch never started a worker).

idle_tick_multiplier.description: Lengthens the tick cadence by this factor when nothing is actively running (all waiting/stalled). 1 = uniform cadence.

task_id.description: Stable id for this run, threaded into the heartbeat and result. A2A-ready: a UUID is recommended.

target_repo.description: Absolute path the worker operates on. Council A-2: absolute, never cwd-derived.

dispatch_mode.description: v1.5.9: 'subagent' (in-session agent Task/Agent tool, cadence rung 1 only) or 'shell' (detached host-CLI subprocess; REQUIRED for cadence rungs 2-4 / the ticker). schema_version stays '1' — 'shell' is an additive enum value.

worker_prompt.description: The worker prompt. MUST contain the {HEARTBEAT_PATH}/{TASK_ID}/{RUN_DIR}/{TARGET_REPO} placeholders (plus {HARNESS_BIN} for the helper/demo-worker location) the tick engine substitutes with absolute paths at dispatch time (Council A-2 / FR-21a — no model-transcribed paths). subagent mode: passed verbatim to the subagent. shell mode: written to queue/job-NNNNN.prompt.txt (quoting/arg-length safety) and referenced by worker_cmd via {PROMPT_FILE}.

heartbeat_path.description: FR-20 (optional): an ABSOLUTE path to a heartbeat file the job already writes. When set, the harness watches THIS file instead of assigning the run-dir default {HEARTBEAT_PATH} (and {HEARTBEAT_PATH} resolves to it for the worker) — so a pre-existing tool with its own status-file location needs no change. The run-dir result/manifest layout is unaffected.

worker_cmd.description: shell mode only: argv template the ticker Popens detached. Tokens may carry the placeholder block PLUS {PROMPT_FILE} (the per-job prompt file). Example: ["claude","--print","--model","sonnet","--append-system-prompt","$(cat {PROMPT_FILE})"] or ["python3","<abs>/harness_demo_worker.py","--heartbeat-path","{HEARTBEAT_PATH}","--task-id","{TASK_ID}","--run-dir","{RUN_DIR}"].

auth_check.description: shell mode only (optional): a cheap argv the ticker runs once before the first shell dispatch to verify the host CLI is present + authenticated (FR-16). A nonzero result marks the entry AUTH_OR_LAUNCH_FAILED with an actionable message instead of silently stalling.
